title: Zero-Shot: Asking Cold
concept: Relying purely on pretrained + instruction-tuned knowledge with no examples in the prompt.

exercises.prompt: As a quick recap, what is the primary role of a prompt when interacting with LLMs?

options.text: Prompts permanently update the model's weights
Prompts are only used for training data

exercises.explain: In LLM engineering, prompts are the primary way we instruct models to perform tasks without changing their underlying weights.

cards.title: No Setup Required
cards.body: Imagine you need a quick answer and don't want to spend time writing out examples of how to format it. Zero-shot prompting lets you get straight to the point by relying completely on the AI's built-in smarts.

cards.title: Zero-Shot Prompting
cards.body: Zero-shot prompting means asking an AI to perform a task without giving it any examples of how to do it. You simply describe the task and expect the model to use its built-in knowledge to solve it.

cards.title: The Trivia Master
cards.body: It's like asking a trivia master a question right out of the blue. You don't hand them a practice sheet first; you just ask, and they immediately dive into their memory to give you the answer.

cards.title: Cold Prompt in Action
cards.body: Instead of writing 'Happy -> Positive, Sad -> Negative, Angry -> ?', you simply write: 'Classify the sentiment of this text: "I love this book!"'. The model outputs 'Positive' without needing to see any prior examples.

cards.title: Is Zero-Shot Too Simple?
cards.body: Many think zero-shot prompts are always low quality. In truth, modern LLMs are so highly instruction-tuned that they can handle complex reasoning, translation, and formatting using zero-shot prompts alone.

cards.title: Zero-Shot vs. Few-Shot
cards.body: Comparing how we structure prompts with and without examples to guide the model's output.

exercises.prompt: What is the core characteristic of zero-shot prompting?

options.text: Providing 3-5 examples of the desired output.
Asking the model to do a task with zero prior examples.
Retraining the model on custom datasets.
Using code to call an API.

exercises.explain: Zero-shot prompting does not include any training examples in the prompt itself; it relies purely on the instructions and the model's pre-trained knowledge.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A zero-shot prompt must contain at least one example of the desired output format.
exercises.explain: Zero-shot prompts do not require any input-output examples. They rely entirely on instructions.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing concept about zero-shot prompting.
exercises.sentence: In zero-shot prompting, you provide no examples to guide the model.
exercises.explain: Zero-shot prompting is defined by the absolute absence of task examples in the prompt, relying entirely on the model's pre-trained knowledge.

exercises.prompt: Which of these prompts is a true zero-shot prompt?

options.text: Text: 'Great product' -> Sentiment: Positive. Text: 'Brokeback' -> Sentiment: Neutral. Text: 'Terrible service' -> Sentiment:
Classify the sentiment of the following text as Positive, Negative, or Neutral. Text: 'The food was average.' Sentiment:
Input: Hello -> Output: Bonjour. Input: Cat -> Output: Chat. Input: Dog -> Output:

exercises.explain: Option B represents a zero-shot prompt because it gives a direct instruction and the input text without showing any examples of how to perform the classification. Options A and C provide examples to guide the model.

exercises.prompt: Why does a zero-shot prompt require highly precise instructions to get a specific output format?

options.text: The model will automatically pull real-time data from the web to answer.
The model relies solely on its pre-existing training to interpret and format the answer.
The model requires at least one example to understand any formatting constraints.
The model will fail to output anything because it has no reference points.

exercises.explain: Zero-shot prompting relies entirely on the model's pre-existing knowledge and instruction-tuning. Because no examples are provided to guide the formatting or reasoning steps, the quality of the output depends heavily on how clear, structured, and explicit your prompt's instructions are.
